desc: When x is zero or negative, the DataFrame is empty and max(df["mystery"]) and min(df["mystery"]) raise ValueError.
x is not type- or range-validated, so values such as strings, NaN, or infinity can cause exceptions or a nonterminating loop.
Broad exception handlers silently discard conversion, aggregation, and flagging failures, allowing corrupted or incomplete results without exposing the cause.
The y and z parameters use shared mutable defaults, creating cross-call state hazards even though the parameters are currently unused.
The y and z parameters are never read, making the function contract misleading and increasing maintenance burden.
Each call mutates GLOBAL_THING and STRANGE_CACHE, making results depend on hidden shared state and making concurrent use unsafe.
The function uses two independent unseeded random-number generators, so identical inputs cannot be reproduced reliably.
The computation unconditionally opens and shows a plot, which can block execution or fail in headless environments and prevents noninteractive reuse.
Created matplotlib figures are never closed, so repeated calls can accumulate figure resources and memory.
Every invocation adds unconditional sleep delays unrelated to its result, unnecessarily degrading throughput.
One function combines data generation, transformation, global caching, summarization, delays, and interactive visualization, making it difficult to test and reuse safely.
The row loop repeatedly performs df.iloc[i] lookups instead of vectorized operations, causing substantial avoidable overhead for large x.
